metadata.title: Guideline on the Clinical Investigation of Recombinant and Human Plasma-Derived Factor VIII Products (Revision 2)

rules.rule_text: Inhibitor development must be studied in previously treated patients (PTPs) with >150 exposure days, suffering from severe haemophilia A with FVIII level <1%.
rules.verbatim_quote: Inhibitor development should be studied in previously treated patients (>150 exposure days, suffering from severe haemophilia A with a factor VIII level < 1%);

rules.rule_text: The modified Nijmegen method of the Bethesda assay must be used for inhibitor testing, with validated testing performed in a central laboratory.
rules.verbatim_quote: The modified Nijmegen method of the Bethesda assay should be used. Validated testing should be performed in a central laboratory;

rules.rule_text: Positive inhibitor results must be confirmed by retesting using a second separately drawn sample in a central laboratory. Sampling timepoints must be recorded and included in the SAE report.
rules.verbatim_quote: In case of positive results for an inhibitor, an inhibitor retesting using a second separately drawn sample as confirmatory measurement should be performed in a central laboratory. The sampling timepoints should be recorded and included in the SAE report.

rules.rule_text: Inhibitor thresholds are defined as >=0.6 BU for a 'low-titre' inhibitor and >5 BU for a 'high-titre' inhibitor.
rules.verbatim_quote: The definitions for thresholds are ≥0.6 BU for "a low titre" inhibitor and >5 BU for a 'high-titre' inhibitor.

rules.rule_text: Neutralising antibodies against FVIII must be classified as a serious adverse event (SAE) and reported using the category 'Important Medical Event'.
rules.verbatim_quote: The occurrence of neutralising antibodies to factor VIII (see chapter 5.3), which is a major complication in haemophilia A treatment, is considered to be a serious adverse event (SAE) and should be recorded and reported as such, using the category "Important Medical Event" and any other applicable.

rules.rule_text: All study protocols must include a hypersensitivity questionnaire/reporting form to collect data on reactions to heterologous proteins (e.g. murine, bovine or hamster origin).
rules.verbatim_quote: Depending on the type of product, the development of hypersensitivity reactions to heterologous proteins (e.g. murine, bovine or hamster origin) may occur, with related adverse events, which should be recorded and reported. All study protocols should include a hypersensitivity questionnaire/reporting form to collect all relevant data in this regard.

rules.rule_text: The minimum number of patients for pre-authorisation clinical trials is 100. At least 200 patients are needed in a post-marketing cohort to achieve 95% probability of detecting inhibitors at 1.5% incidence.
rules.verbatim_quote: The number of patients typically needed to be enrolled into the pre-authorisation clinical trials is a minimum of 100. ... In case inhibitors occur at an incidence of 1.5% or higher in PTPs, there is at least 95% probability to observe antibodies in one or more patients in a cohort of 200 patients.

rules.rule_text: Clinical development must follow a stepwise approach: adults/older children first (PTPs >=12 years), then children 0-<12 years only after PK and efficacy/safety data from 20 PTPs >=12 years for at least 50 EDs are available.
rules.verbatim_quote: The clinical development for factor VIII products should follow a stepwise approach in order to have some experience in adults and older children before investigating younger children. Therefore, the initial age cohort to be investigated is PTPs ≥12 years of age. Subsequently, when PK and efficacy/safety data from 20 PTPs ≥12 years for at least 50 EDs are available, the clinical trial(s) in children 0 - <12 years can be initiated.

rules.rule_text: Potency assignments for FVIII products covered by Ph. Eur. monographs must be performed with the Ph. Eur. chromogenic assay.
rules.verbatim_quote: The potency assignments for factor VIII products covered by European Pharmacopoeia (Ph. Eur.) monographs have to be performed with the Ph. Eur chromogenic assay.
rules.exceptions: With agreement of the competent authority, alternative methods of analysis may be used for control purposes, provided that the methods used enable an unequivocal decision to be made.

rules.rule_text: PTPs in the efficacy study must be >=12 years of age, have FVIII level <1%, be immunocompetent (HIV positive patients must have CD4 >200/ul), and have >150 EDs to previous products.
rules.verbatim_quote: Previously treated patients (PTPs) with at least 150 treatment EDs to previous products are considered as low risk patients and should be evaluated for product related immunogenicity. These PTPs should be ≥12 years of age, with a factor VIII level <1% and immunocompetent (HIV positive patients should have CD4 lymphocytes >200/μl). The patients should be HIV negative or have a viral load < 200 particles/μl ~400000 copies/ml.

rules.rule_text: PK trial must be performed in at least 12 PTPs, recording incremental recovery, in vivo half-life, AUC, and clearance, in patients without inhibitors who are not actively bleeding.
rules.verbatim_quote: A pharmacokinetic trial should be performed in at least 12 PTPs (details of patient inclusion criteria see previous paragraph "choice of patients"). The study should record incremental recovery, in vivo half-life, area under the curve (AUC), and clearance in patients without inhibitors who are not actively bleeding.

rules.rule_text: Patients must not have received any FVIII product for at least 4 days before PK sampling. Existing PK information from patient's previous FVIII product must be available prior to first administration of the new product.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients should not have received an infusion of any factor VIII product for at least 4 days. In order to allow for evaluation of a patient's individual response, existing pharmacokinetic information with the patient's previous factor VIII product (historical or recent recovery and half-life) should be available prior to first administration of the new factor VIII product.

rules.rule_text: PK samples must be taken before injection of 25-50 IU/kg, at 10-15 minutes (baseline), 30 minutes, 1 hour post-infusion, and at additional timepoints 3, 6, 9, 24, 28, and 32 hours. A 48h sample is optional if >=50 IU/kg was given.
rules.verbatim_quote: Samples should be taken before injection of 25-50 IU/kg of the factor VIII product (baseline), 10-15 minutes (times refer to the interval after the completion of the infusion) and at 30 minutes, and 1 hour. Additional time points to include 3, 6, 9, 24, 28, and 32 hours post infusion; a 48 hour sample is optional provided the patient was given at least 50 IU/kg.
rules.exceptions: Depending on the type of factor VIII product (e.g. prolonged half-life) sampling time points may be adjusted to cover the main parts of the activity time profile.

rules.rule_text: At least 3 different lots must be employed in the PK trial. Incremental recovery is determined as the peak factor level recorded in the first hour after infusion, reported as [IU/ml]/[IU/kg].
rules.verbatim_quote: At least 3 different lots should be employed in the trial. Incremental recovery is determined as the peak factor level recorded in the first hour after infusion and is reported as [IU/ml]/[IU/kg].

rules.rule_text: The same assay must be used for analysis of both the product and the patient's plasma. Preferably the chromogenic assay should be used.
rules.verbatim_quote: Preferably, the same assay should be used for analysis of the product and the patient's plasma (see also 6.1.1).

rules.rule_text: PK data must be described according to body weight categories (normal range, overweight, underweight).
rules.verbatim_quote: An additional description of the pharmacokinetic data according to body weight (normal range, overweight and underweight) should be provided.

rules.rule_text: PK patients must be re-tested for the same PK parameters after 3-6 months using the same dose. Inhibitor testing must also be performed.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients taking part in the pharmacokinetic trial should continue treatment with the product, and should be re-tested for the same pharmacokinetic parameters after 3-6 months using the same dose as in the first investigation. Inhibitor testing should also be performed (see Annex III for further details).

rules.rule_text: If the product is marketed in different strengths with a broad range of FVIII concentrations, PK of the lowest and highest concentrations after reconstitution must be investigated.
rules.verbatim_quote: If a factor VIII product should be marketed in different strengths leading to a broad range of factor VIII concentrations after reconstitution, the pharmacokinetics of the lowest and highest concentration should be investigated unless otherwise justified.

rules.rule_text: Clinical efficacy must be evaluated in at least 50 PTPs with severe haemophilia A, with a minimum observation period of 50 exposure days. Response must be assessed as 'none', 'moderate', 'good', or 'excellent'.
rules.source_section: 6.2 Efficacy in PTPs >=12 years - Efficacy including surgery
rules.verbatim_quote: Clinical efficacy of factor VIII should be evaluated in at least 50 PTPs, suffering from severe haemophilia A, and who are immunocompetent (inclusion criteria see paragraph "choice of patients"). During an observation period of a minimum of 50 exposure days, clinical response should be assessed by the patients. Response should be assessed as "none", "moderate", "good" or "excellent" by the physician for those patients who were treated in hospital with the product for major bleeds.

rules.rule_text: Surgical efficacy must be evaluated in a minimum of 5 patients undergoing at least 10 surgical procedures (comprising major surgeries), assessing haemostasis, blood loss, and transfusion requirements.
rules.source_section: 6.2 Efficacy in PTPs >=12 years - Efficacy including surgery
rules.verbatim_quote: response should be determined by the physician in a minimum of 5 patients undergoing at least 10 surgical procedures (comprising major surgeries), including efficacy of haemostasis, loss of blood, and requirements for transfusion.

rules.rule_text: For long-term prophylaxis claims, patients must be treated for 6 months and assessed for bleeding episodes, bleeding intervals, and number of treatments.
rules.source_section: 6.2 Efficacy in PTPs >=12 years - Efficacy including surgery
rules.verbatim_quote: For the assessment of clinical efficacy of factor VIII in long-term prophylaxis, patients should be treated for 6 months and assessed for bleeding episodes, bleeding intervals and number of treatments.

rules.rule_text: If continuous infusion therapy is claimed, the study must include at least 12 severe haemophilia A patients (FVIII <1%) undergoing elective major surgical procedures.
rules.source_section: 6.2 Efficacy in PTPs >=12 years - Continuous infusion
rules.verbatim_quote: If continuous infusion therapy is claimed, the study should be carried out in at least 12 severe haemophilia A patients (factor VIII <1%) undergoing elective major surgical procedures.

rules.rule_text: For continuous infusion, individual PK analysis must be performed pre-surgery to estimate clearance. After initial 24h, clearance must be recalculated daily using steady state equation.
rules.source_section: 6.2 Efficacy in PTPs >=12 years - Continuous infusion
rules.verbatim_quote: Prior to surgery, a pharmacokinetic analysis in each individual should be performed to obtain, in particular, an estimate of clearance. ... After the initial 24 hours of continuous infusion, the clearance should be calculated again every day using the steady state equation with the measured level and the known rate of infusion.

rules.rule_text: Inhibitor sampling in clinical studies must be performed not less than 3 days after the previous administration, when FVIII level has reached baseline. Inhibitor titre must be reported in Bethesda Units using the Nijmegen modification.
rules.source_section: 6.2 Efficacy in PTPs >=12 years - Immunogenicity testing
rules.verbatim_quote: In the clinical studies, it is proposed to perform sampling for inhibitor measurements not less than 3 days after the previous administration, if possible. ... The titre of the inhibitor should be reported in Bethesda Units (BU) using the Nijmegen modification of the Bethesda assay.
rules.exceptions: Product specific properties e.g. extended half-life should be taken into account to avoid interference from residual factor VIII product.

rules.rule_text: Plasma samples from patients suspected of or confirmed to have developed inhibitors must be stored until evaluation by the competent authority is completed, for possible additional inhibitor analysis.
rules.source_section: 6.2 Efficacy in PTPs >=12 years - Immunogenicity testing
rules.verbatim_quote: Plasma samples from patients who are suspected of inhibitors or who have developed inhibitors should be stored until the evaluation of the clinical study by the competent authority is completed in order to permit additional inhibitor analysis if needed.

rules.rule_text: Pre-treatment serum sample from each patient must be stored at -70 degrees C for possible future viral safety testing.
rules.source_section: 6.2 Efficacy in PTPs >=12 years - Viral safety
rules.verbatim_quote: A pre-treatment serum sample from each patient included in the clinical trials should be stored at -70°C for possible future testing.

rules.rule_text: Paediatric trial must include at least 50 children in two age cohorts: 25 PTPs aged 6-<12 years, and 25 patients <6 years with >50 EDs to previous FVIII products.
rules.verbatim_quote: a multicentre trial should include at least 50 children allocated to two age cohorts. A minimum of 25 patients should be PTPs (>150 ED) at the age of 6-<12 years and at least 25 patients should be <6 years who have undergone >50 EDs with previous factor VIII products.

rules.rule_text: The paediatric trial in children <12 years must not start before safety is proven for 50 EDs each in 20 patients included in the PTP trial >=12 years.
rules.verbatim_quote: The clinical trial in children < 12 years should not start before safety is proven for 50 EDs each of 20 patients who are included in the PTP trial ≥12 years.

rules.rule_text: PK in children must be investigated in 12 patients of each age cohort, recording incremental recovery, in vivo half-life, AUC, and clearance. Reduced sampling timepoints are acceptable (baseline, 1h, 10h, 24h, 48h).
rules.verbatim_quote: The clinical trial in children should begin with the investigation of pharmacokinetics (incremental recovery, in vivo half-life, AUC and clearance) in 12 patients of each age cohort. ... PK sampling time points can be reduced to measurements prior to infusion (baseline) and 1 hour, 10 hours, 24 hours and 48 hours after infusion.
rules.exceptions: Depending on the type of factor VIII product (e.g. prolonged half-life) further sampling time points could be necessary.

rules.rule_text: The paediatric study must continue until patients have received a minimum of 50 EDs. Inhibitor testing schedule must follow Annex III (immediately before first exposure, ED10-15, ED50-75, and if there is any suspicion of development).
rules.verbatim_quote: the study in children should continue until the patients have received a minimum of 50 EDs to the investigational product. ... Inhibitor testing should be performed following the same testing schedule as set out in Annex III

rules.rule_text: Formal PUP (previously untreated patients) clinical trials are not required for marketing authorisation. However, every PUP must be closely monitored via a well-defined disease Registry.
rules.verbatim_quote: the obligation to perform clinical trials in PUPs for marketing authorisation purposes has been deleted. ... Therefore, formal PUP studies are not required; however, every PUP should be closely monitored with regards to treatment performance and inhibitor development through a well-defined and well-managed disease Registry.

rules.rule_text: Post-marketing investigation must enroll 200 PTPs followed for 100 EDs each. The study protocol must be submitted as part of the RMP (GVP module V). The study must be completed within 4 years.
rules.verbatim_quote: The number of patients typically needed in a post-marketing study with a factor VIII product to cover especially immunogenicity aspects (besides general efficacy and safety) is 200. ... The post-marketing investigation should be completed within 4 years.
rules.exceptions: In case of plasma-derived factor VIII products (e.g. manufactured by known methods, for national approval only) a smaller number of patients could be enrolled but justification should be provided.

rules.rule_text: A progress study report must be provided to competent authorities 2 years after marketing authorisation for evaluation of recruitment status, progress, and timeline adherence.
rules.verbatim_quote: A separate progress study report should be provided to the relevant Competent Authority(ies) 2 years after marketing authorisation to allow for evaluation of recruitment status, progress and the adherence to timelines.

rules.rule_text: For manufacturing process changes, a comparative PK trial with pre-change vs post-change product must be performed in at least 12 PTPs with haemophilia A (FVIII <1%). The immunogenicity profile of the post-change product must be assessed.
rules.source_section: 7. Change in the manufacturing process / 7.2 Efficacy
rules.verbatim_quote: A comparative pharmacokinetic trial with "pre-change" product versus the "post-change" product should be performed in at least 12 PTPs suffering from haemophilia A (factor VIII <1%). ... Of special interest will be whether the immunogenicity profile of the "post-change" product remains the same when compared to the "pre-change" product.
rules.exceptions: The extent of clinical data to be provided has to be judged on a case by case basis depending on the anticipated impact of the changes.

rules.rule_text: Both PUPs and PTPs must be encouraged to enroll in disease-specific registries. A core data set is defined including administrative, demographic, anamnestic, treatment, inhibitor, and concomitant event information.
rules.verbatim_quote: every patient suffering from haemophilia, both PUPs and PTPs should be encouraged to enroll in disease specific registries. ... Since a variety of haemophilia registries exist on national and international level a core parameter set is essential allowing for potential data merging and analysis and is proposed thereafter.
